0.body:
Define simple completions using a spec file (based on carapace ). name: mycmd
description: my command
flags: --optarg?: optarg flag -r, --repeatable*: repeatable flag -v=: flag with value
persistentflags: --help: bool flag
completion: flag: optarg: ["one", "two\twith description", "three\twith style\tblue"] v: ["$files"]
commands:
- name: sub description: subcommand completion: positional: - ["$list(,)", "1", "2", "3"] - ["$directories"]
0.title: carapace-spec

1.body:
# bash
source <(carapace-spec example.yaml) # elvish
eval (carapace-spec example.yaml|slurp) # fish
carapace-spec example.yaml | source # oil
source <(carapace-spec example.yaml) # nushell
carapace-spec example.yaml | save example.nu ; nu -c 'source example.nu' # powershell
carapace-spec example.yaml | Out-String | Invoke-Expression # tcsh
eval `carapace-spec example.yaml` # xonsh
exec($(carapace-spec example.yaml)) # zsh
source <(carapace-spec example.yaml)
1.title: Usage

12.body: Values are defined with an optional description and optional style delimited by \t. ["value", "value\tdescription", "value\tdescription\tblue"]
12.title: Values

13.body: Macros are basically Actions exposed to the spec (E.g. $files([.go, go.mod]) . The brackets are optional if no argument is passed (so $files is equivalent to $files()).
13.title: Macros

16.body: $(<command>) executes given command in a sh shell. ["$(echo -e 'a\nb\nc')"]
16.title: execcommand

17.body: $files([<suffixes>]) completes files with an optional list of suffixes to filter on. ["$files([.go, go.mod, go.sum])"]
17.title: files

18.body: $message(<message>) adds given error message to completion. ["$message(some error)"]
18.title: message

19.body: $spec(<file>) completes arguments using the given spec file. This implicitly disables flag parsing for the corresponding (sub)command. ["$spec(example.yaml)"]
19.title: spec

2.body:
type Command struct { Name string Description string Flags map[string]string PersistentFlags map[string]string Completion struct { Flag map[string][]string Positional [][]string PositionalAny []string Dash [][]string DashAny []string } Commands []Command
}
2.title: Command

20.body: Modifiers change the completion for a position in general.
20.title: Modifier

22.body: $list(<delimiter>) completes values as unique list with given delimiter. ["$list(,)", "a", "b", "c", "d"]
22.title: list

23.body: $multiparts(<delimiter>) completes values splitted on given delimiter separately. ["$multiparts(/)", "a", "a/b", "a/c", "b", "b/a"]
23.title: multiparts

24.body: $nospace prevents space suffix being added to the inserted values. ["$nospace", "one", "two"]
24.title: nospace

25.body: $noflag disables flag parsing for the current command. ["$noflag"]
25.title: noflag

26.body:
Custom macros can be added with AddMacro (names are prefixed with $_). // `$_noarg` without argument
AddMacro("noarg", MacroN(func() carapace.Action { return carapace.ActionValues()})) // `$_arg({name: example, enabled: true})` with argument (primitive or struct)
AddMacro("arg", MacroI(func(u User) carapace.Action { return carapace.ActionValues()})) // `$_vararg([another, example])` with variable arguments (primitive or struct)
AddMacro("vararg", MacroV(func(s ...string) carapace.Action { return carapace.ActionValues()})) Arguments are parsed as yaml so only struct keys deviating from the default need to be set.
26.title: Custom

27.title: Default (experimental)

28.body:
Variables are replaced using drone/envsubst . ${C_ARG<position>} positional values [0..n] ${C_FLAG_<flagname>} flag values (if modified) ${C_PART<position>} parts of the current word during multipart completion [0..n] ${C_CALLBACK} the word currently being completed name: myvar
flags: --suffix=: file suffixes
completion: flag: suffix: ["$list(,)", ".go", "go.sum", "go.mod", ".md", "LICENSE"] positional: - ["$files([${C_FLAG_SUFFIX//,/, }])"] # replace `,` with `, ` for valid array syntax - ["${C_FLAG_SUFFIX:-default}", "${C_ARG0}"] # use default if flag is not set
28.title: Variables

3.body: Flags are defined as a map of name and description. The name can contain shorthand, longhand and modifiers matching this regex . flags: -b: bool flag -v=: shorthand with value --repeatable*: longhand repeatable -o, --optarg?: shorthand and longhand with optional argument
3.title: Flags

4.body: = flag takes an argument * flag is repeatable ? flag takes an optional argument
4.title: Modifiers:

5.body: Same as Flags , but passes them down to subcommands.
5.title: PersistentFlags

6.body: Completion for a position is defined by an array of static Values and Macros .
6.title: Completion

8.body: Positional completion is defined by an array
8.title: Positional
